title: Build a cross-tab banner workbook with pairwise column significance tests
summary: Cross-tabulates every variable in the input file against the banner variables and writes the result to Excel. Categorical rows get proportion z-tests between banner columns; continuous rows get weighted t-tests. Optional case weights are honoured throughout.
description: The banner is the Cartesian cross of the `banner_vars` categories plus a Total column. For each variable in the input file, a table is produced: categorical variables emit one row per value with a weighted (or unweighted) percentage per banner column and a significance-letters cell indicating which other columns this column is significantly higher/lower than at the standard alpha; continuous variables emit a mean and standard error per banner column with pairwise t-test letters. Missing-coded values are excluded by default — set `include_na = true` to keep them in the base. Multi-response / checkbox variables produce one row per option; set `include_unchecked = true` to also emit the implied unchecked complement.
usage:
[["spss-reader::create-table"]]
input_path = "./data/survey.sav"
output_dir = "./output"
banner_vars = ["Q_gender", "Q_age_group"]
weight_var = "weight"
include_na = false

arguments.name: output_dir
arguments.description: Directory for the banner-table .xlsx; created if missing.

arguments.name: banner_vars
arguments.description: Variables whose categories form the table columns. Empty = Total column only.

arguments.name: weight_var
arguments.description: Optional weight variable. When supplied, counts and tests use weighted values.

arguments.name: discrete_count_cutoff
arguments.description: Variables with fewer distinct values than this cutoff are treated as categorical, others as continuous.

arguments.name: include_na
arguments.description: Keep missing-coded values in the base when true.

arguments.name: include_unchecked
arguments.description: For multi-response variables, also emit unchecked-complement rows when true.

notes: Significance letters follow the standard banner-testing convention: a letter in a cell marks a banner column whose value this cell is significantly higher than. Direction and alpha are fixed defaults inside the plugin.
Banner columns with n < the plugin's minimum-base threshold are marked low-base and excluded from pairwise tests, not omitted from the table.
Weighted tests use the survey-design variance approximation appropriate for the supplied weights — results are not identical to unweighted tests on the same data.

artifacts.title: Cross-tab banner workbook
artifacts.description: An Excel workbook with one table per variable showing weighted percentages (or means for continuous variables) broken out across banner columns. Significance-letter cells flag statistically significant differences between banner groups. This is the standard frequency-table deliverable format for quantitative market research.
